name: Strawberry Mousse
recipeInstructions:
1. Lightly oil a 1-quart ring mold, charlotte mold, or bowl.
2. Puree the strawberries in a food processor or blender until yielding at least 1 cup of puree. Set aside.
3. In a small heatproof pan, microwave dish, or metal measure, combine lemon juice, water, and gelatin. Let stand for 5 minutes or until spongy.
4. In a heatproof bowl, mix eggs, egg yolk, and sugar with an electric mixer or whisk over nearly boiling water until thick and light in color.
5. Remove from the heat and continue beating until cool.
6. Dissolve gelatin over low heat or microwave, ensuring all granules are dissolved.
7. Stir the liquid gelatin into the egg mixture.
8. Add the strawberry puree to the mixture.
9. Place the entire mixture over a pan of ice water and stir with a rubber spatula until setting.
10. Fold in softly whipped cream.
11. Pour mousse into the prepared mold and chill until set, 3-4 hours or overnight.
12. Lightly oil a serving plate. Tilt mousse from mold to catch air bubbles, then invert onto the plate.
13. Shake lightly and remove mold.
14. Pipe rosettes of stiff whipped cream and place strawberries on top.
15. Can be made up to 1 day in advance.
